Photo by: AI-generated imageSmall styling improvements can make a big difference when you combine GeneratePress with Gutenberg.
A clean WordPress website does not always require a heavy page builder or complicated design system.
With GeneratePress and Gutenberg, you can create fast, modern, and professional-looking layouts using a simple workflow. The key is knowing how to style your blocks properly and maintain consistency throughout your website.
Here are some practical GeneratePress styling tricks that can help you improve your Gutenberg block designs.
1. Start with a Consistent Color System
One of the easiest ways to improve a website’s appearance is to use a consistent color palette.
Instead of choosing different colors for every Gutenberg block, select a few primary colors for your website:

• A primary brand color

• A secondary accent color

• A background color

• A text color

• A subtle border color
This creates a more unified visual experience.
For example, your buttons, links, headings, and highlighted sections should feel like they belong to the same design system.
Consistency is often more important than complexity.
2. Use Spacing to Create a Cleaner Layout
Many websites look crowded because there is not enough space between different elements.
Gutenberg blocks give you plenty of flexibility when it comes to spacing. You can use padding and margins to create better separation between:

• Headings and paragraphs

• Images and text

• Different content sections

• Cards and buttons

• Sections with different backgrounds
However, spacing should be balanced.
Too little spacing makes a layout feel crowded, while too much spacing can make the content feel disconnected.
The goal is to create a layout where every element has enough room to breathe.
3. Improve Typography for Better Readability
Typography can completely change the appearance of a Gutenberg layout.
When using GeneratePress, pay attention to:
Font size
Font weight
Line height
Heading spacing
Paragraph width
Your headings should be easy to scan, while your body text should be comfortable to read.
A good typography system also helps create a visual hierarchy. Readers should immediately understand which text is the main heading, which is a subheading, and which is supporting content.
This is especially important for long-form blog posts.
4. Use Group Blocks to Organize Content
The Group block is one of the most useful tools in Gutenberg.
You can place multiple blocks inside a Group block and style them as a single section.
For example, a Group block can contain:
A heading
A paragraph
An image
A button
You can then add background colors, padding, borders, or other styling to the entire group.
This is particularly useful for creating feature sections, promotional areas, content boxes, and call-to-action sections.
5. Create Better Sections with Stack and Row Layouts
Modern websites often use structured layouts instead of placing every block one below another.
Gutenberg’s layout blocks can help you create:
Two-column sections
Feature layouts
Content and image combinations
Service cards
Call-to-action sections
The important thing is to keep the layout simple.
A well-structured layout with a few carefully selected blocks can look much better than a page filled with unnecessary design elements.
6. Use Background Colors Carefully
Background colors are a simple way to make important sections stand out.
For example, you can use a subtle background color for:
A featured content section
A newsletter area
A call-to-action block
A special announcement
A group of related features
The key word is subtle.
Using too many bright colors can make a website look inconsistent. A simple background color with proper spacing is often enough to make a section visually distinct.
7. Keep Borders and Border Radius Consistent
Cards and content boxes often look better when they have a consistent visual style.
If you use rounded corners on one group of cards, try to use a similar style for other related cards.
The same applies to:
Border thickness
Border color
Corner radius
Shadow effects
A consistent design system makes the website feel more polished and intentional.
8. Use Shadows Carefully
A small shadow can help separate a card or section from the background.
But excessive shadows can make a website look outdated or visually heavy.
Instead of adding shadows to every block, use them only where they serve a purpose.
For example, a subtle shadow can work well for:
Feature cards
Pricing sections
Floating content boxes
Important call-to-action areas
Simple styling usually works best with GeneratePress.
9. Design for Mobile First
A Gutenberg layout may look perfect on a desktop screen but appear crowded on mobile.
Always check:
Font sizes
Padding
Column layouts
Button sizes
Image widths
Section spacing
If a two-column layout feels too tight on mobile, it may need to stack vertically.
Responsive design is not an optional feature anymore. A large portion of website visitors use mobile devices, so every Gutenberg section should be tested on smaller screens.
10. Keep Your Design Simple
One of the biggest advantages of GeneratePress is its lightweight approach.
You do not need to add complicated animations, excessive effects, or too many design elements to create a professional website.
A simple combination of:
Good typography
Consistent colors
Proper spacing
Clean layouts
Responsive design
can produce excellent results.
The best designs are often the ones that make the content easier to understand.
Final Thoughts
GeneratePress and Gutenberg provide a powerful combination for creating fast and professional WordPress websites.
You do not need a complicated page builder to create attractive layouts. With the right approach to spacing, typography, colors, layout blocks, and responsive design, even simple Gutenberg blocks can look modern and polished.
The real secret is consistency.
When every section follows the same visual language, your website feels more professional, easier to navigate, and more enjoyable to use.
What is your favorite way to style Gutenberg blocks with GeneratePress?
